{{Administrative agency | Country = [[File:Flag of Sendai, Miyagi.svg|20px|border]] [[Sendai City]] | Official name = Sendai City Board of Education | Insignia = | Insignia size = | Insignia caption = | Chief title = [[Superintendent of Education]] | Chief name = Hiroyuki Fukuda | Deputy chief title = Acting Superintendent | Deputy chief name = Koji Hanabuchi | Parent agency = | Parent agency overview = | Sub-agency 1 = | Sub-agency 1 overview = | Headquarters = 1-5-12 Kamisugi, Aoba-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan (14F, Kamisugi Branch Office) | Staff = | Annual budget = | Fiscal year = | Established = | Renamed = | Predecessor = | Website = [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website] | Other = }}
'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref>
== See Also == * [[Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]

revision.text:
{{Administrative agency | Country = [[File:Flag of Sendai, Miyagi.svg|20px|border]] [[Sendai City]] | Official name = Sendai City Board of Education | Insignia = | Insignia size = | Insignia caption = | Chief title = [[Superintendent of Education]] | Chief name = Hiroyuki Fukuda | Deputy chief title = Acting Superintendent | Deputy chief name = Koji Hanabuchi | Parent agency = | Parent agency overview = | Sub-agency 1 = | Sub-agency 1 overview = | Headquarters = 1-5-12 Kamisugi, Aoba-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan (14F, Kamisugi Branch Office) | Staff = | Annual budget = | Fiscal year = | Established = | Renamed = | Predecessor = | Website = [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website] | Other = }}
'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref>== See Also == * [[Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]

{{Administrative agency | Country = [[File:Flag of Sendai, Miyagi.svg|20px|border]] [[Sendai City]] | Official name = Sendai City Board of Education | Insignia = | Insignia size = | Insignia caption = | Chief title = [[Superintendent of Education]] | Chief name = Hiroyuki Fukuda | Deputy chief title = Acting Superintendent | Deputy chief name = Koji Hanabuchi | Parent agency = | Parent agency overview = | Sub-agency 1 = | Sub-agency 1 overview = | Headquarters = 1-5-12 Kamisugi, Aoba-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan (14F, Kamisugi Branch Office) | Staff = | Annual budget = | Fiscal year = | Established = | Renamed = | Predecessor = | Website = [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website] | Other = }}
'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]

revision.text:
{{Administrative agency | Country = [[File:Flag of Sendai, Miyagi.svg|20px|border]] [[Sendai City]] | Official name = Sendai City Board of Education | Insignia = | Insignia size = | Insignia caption = | Chief title = [[Superintendent of Education]] | Chief name = Hiroyuki Fukuda | Deputy chief title = Acting Superintendent | Deputy chief name = Koji Hanabuchi | Parent agency = | Parent agency overview = | Sub-agency 1 = | Sub-agency 1 overview = | Headquarters = 1-5-12 Kamisugi, Aoba-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan (14F, Kamisugi Branch Office) | Staff = | Annual budget = | Fiscal year = | Established = | Renamed = | Predecessor = | Website = [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website] | Other = }}
'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]

revision.text:
{{Administrative agency | Country = [[File:Flag of Sendai, Miyagi.svg|20px|border]] [[Sendai City]] | Official name = Sendai City Board of Education | Insignia = | Insignia size = | Insignia caption = | Chief title = [[Superintendent of Education]] | Chief name = Hiroyuki Fukuda | Deputy chief title = Acting Superintendent | Deputy chief name = Koji Hanabuchi | Parent agency = | Parent agency overview = | Sub-agency 1 = | Sub-agency 1 overview = | Headquarters = 1-5-12 Kamisugi, Aoba-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan (14F, Kamisugi Branch Office) | Staff = | Annual budget = | Fiscal year = | Established = | Renamed = | Predecessor = | Website = [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website] | Other = }}
'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]
{{Local boards of education in Japan}} {{education-stub}}

'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]

Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[:en:Miyagi University of Education|Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[:en:Sendai University|Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]

'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[:en:Miyagi University of Education|Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[:en:Sendai University|Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:仙台市教育委員会]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11377846]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Administrative agency | Country = [[File:Flag of Sendai, Miyagi.svg|20px|border]] [[Sendai City]] | Official name = Sendai City Board of Education | Insignia = | Insignia size = | Insignia caption = | Chief title = [[Superintendent of Education]] | Chief name = Hiroyuki Fukuda | Deputy chief title = Acting Superintendent | Deputy chief name = Koji Hanabuchi | Parent agency = | Parent agency overview = | Sub-agency 1 = | Sub-agency 1 overview = | Headquarters = 1-5-12 Kamisugi, Aoba-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan (14F, Kamisugi Branch Office) | Staff = | Annual budget = | Fiscal year = | Established = | Renamed = | Predecessor = | Website = [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website] | Other = }}
'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[:en:Miyagi University of Education|Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[:en:Sendai University|Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]

Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[:en:Miyagi University of Education|Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[:en:Sendai University|Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:仙台市教育委員会]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:仙台市教育委員会]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:仙台市教育委員会]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11377846]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Administrative agency | Country = [[File:Flag of Sendai, Miyagi.svg|20px|border]] [[Sendai City]] | Official name = Sendai City Board of Education | Insignia = | Insignia size = | Insignia caption = | Chief title = [[Superintendent of Education]] | Chief name = Hiroyuki Fukuda | Deputy chief title = Acting Superintendent | Deputy chief name = Koji Hanabuchi | Parent agency = | Parent agency overview = | Sub-agency 1 = | Sub-agency 1 overview = | Headquarters = 1-5-12 Kamisugi, Aoba-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan (14F, Kamisugi Branch Office) | Staff = | Annual budget = | Fiscal year = | Established = | Renamed = | Predecessor = | Website = [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website] | Other = }}
'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as [[:en:Miyagi University of Education|Miyagi University of Education]], as well as students from [[:en:Sendai University|Sendai University]] who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
== Notes == <references />
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website]

The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]

{{Administrative agency | Country = [[File:Flag of Sendai, Miyagi.svg|20px|border]] [[Sendai City]] | Official name = Sendai City Board of Education | Insignia = | Insignia size = | Insignia caption = | Chief title = [[Superintendent of Education]] | Chief name = Hiroyuki Fukuda | Deputy chief title = Acting Superintendent | Deputy chief name = Koji Hanabuchi | Parent agency = | Parent agency overview = | Sub-agency 1 = | Sub-agency 1 overview = | Headquarters = 1-5-12 Kamisugi, Aoba-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan (14F, Kamisugi Branch Office) | Staff = | Annual budget = | Fiscal year = | Established = | Renamed = | Predecessor = | Website = [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/ Sendai City Board of Education Official Website] | Other = }}
'''Sendai City Board of Education''' (仙台市教育委員会) is a municipal administrative body located in the Kamisugi Branch Office of Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture. It oversees education-related matters within Sendai City.
== Overview == The organization is divided into various departments, including the General Affairs and Planning Department, Educational Personnel Department, School Education Department, and Lifelong Learning Department, each further subdivided into specific sections based on their operations. The board conducts activities such as education committee meetings, implementing surveys on bullying prevention, and handling school mergers and establishments.
The administrative functions of the Board of Education are carried out by the "Sendai City Board of Education Secretariat," which operates under the City’s Education Bureau.
The board oversees all elementary and junior high schools in the city, as well as several schools not under the jurisdiction of Miyagi Prefecture, such as [[Sendai Seiryo Secondary School]], [[Sendai Special Needs School]], [[Sendai High School]], [[Sendai Commercial High School]], [[Sendai Technical High School]], and [[Sendai Taishi High School]].
Since 2004, the board has implemented the "Student Support Staff Program," which dispatches university student volunteers to assist teachers in elementary and junior high schools. Participants include students aspiring to become teachers from institutions such as {{ill|Miyagi University of Education|en|Miyagi University of Education|WD=Q1888278|lt=Miyagi University of Education|1=Miyagi University of Education}}, as well as students from {{ill|Sendai University|en|Sendai University|WD=Q7450188|lt=Sendai University|1=Sendai University}} who assist with extracurricular activities.<ref>[http://www.kahoku.co.jp/tohokunews/201503/20150316_11016.html University Students, Teachers, and Children Benefit: 10 Years of Dispatch Programs] - Kahoku Shimpo</ref>
== Bullying Issues == Sendai City has faced criticism for its handling of bullying-related incidents, with several tragic events occurring in recent years. On April 26, 2017, a second-year junior high school student committed suicide by jumping, leading to further scrutiny of the board's response to bullying. Notably, during the tenure of the current superintendent, there have been three suicide cases related to bullying.<ref>http://www.media-japan.info/?p=4318</ref><ref>https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/national/article/325122</ref><ref>http://ox-tv.jp/nc/smp/article.aspx?d=20170206&no=33</ref> * In September 2014, a first-year junior high school student from [[Izumi Ward, Sendai|Izumi Ward]] committed suicide due to bullying at [[Tate Junior High School]]. The school falsely stated that the student had transferred, and the board delayed acknowledging the suicide for almost a year.
In December 2021, a first-grade elementary school boy at a Sendai municipal school was subjected to violent bullying, including being beaten and kicked by classmates. The school dismissed the incident as "a popular game" and denied it was bullying. However, the boy’s parents escalated the matter to the board, which designated the case as a "serious incident."<ref>[https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220314-OYT1T50198/ First-Grade Boy Beaten and Kicked by Classmates: School Dismisses Incident as 'Popular Game'] Yomiuri Shimbun, March 14, 2022</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] * [[Miyagi Prefecture Board of Education]]
